description:
This function is intended for debugging and introspection. It ignores its argument and always returns 1. The arguments are not evaluated.
But during index analysis, the argument of this function is assumed to be not wrapped in indexHint . This allows to select data in index ranges by the corresponding condition but without further filtering by this condition. The index in ClickHouse is sparse and using indexHint will yield more data than specifying the same condition directly.
detail:
[DESCRIPTION]: This function is intended for debugging and introspection. It ignores its argument and always returns 1. The arguments are not evaluated.
But during index analysis, the argument of this function is assumed to be not wrapped in indexHint . This allows to select data in index ranges by the corresponding condition but without further filtering by this condition. The index in ClickHouse is sparse and using indexHint will yield more data than specifying the same condition directly.<sep>[SYNTAX]: SELECT * FROM table WHERE indexHint(<expression>)<sep>[RETURN]: - 1 . Uint8 .<sep>[DEMO]: в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җkв”Җв”¬в”Җcount()в”Җв”җ ; в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җkв”Җв”¬в”Җcount()в”Җв”җ ; SELECT

description: Calculates the result of an aggregate function based on a single value. This function can be used to initialize aggregate functions with combinator -State . You can create states of aggregate functions and insert them to columns of type AggregateFunction or use initialized aggregates as default values.
detail:
[DESCRIPTION]: Calculates the result of an aggregate function based on a single value. This function can be used to initialize aggregate functions with combinator -State . You can create states of aggregate functions and insert them to columns of type AggregateFunction or use initialized aggregates as default values.<sep>[SYNTAX]: initializeAggregation (aggregate_function, arg1, arg2, ..., argN)<sep>[ARGUMENTS]: - aggregate_function вҖ” Name of the aggregation function to initialize. String .
- arg вҖ” Arguments of aggregate function.
Returned value(s)
- Result of aggregation for every row passed to the function.
The return type is the same as the return type of function, that initializeAggregation takes as first argument.<sep>[DEMO]: SELECT uniqMerge(state) FROM (SELECT initializeAggregation('uniqState', number % 3) AS state FROM numbers(10000)); ; в”Ңв”ҖuniqMerge(state)в”Җв”җ ; SELECT finalizeAggregation(state), toTypeName(state) FROM (SELECT initializeAggregation('sumState', number % 3) AS state FROM numbers(5));
example: SELECT uniqMerge(state) FROM (SELECT initializeAggregation('uniqState', number % 3) AS state FROM numbers(10000));
SELECT finalizeAggregation(state), toTypeName(state) FROM (SELECT initializeAggregation('sumState', number % 3) AS state FROM numbers(5));

в”Ңв”ҖfinalizeAggregation(state)в”Җв”¬в”ҖtoTypeName(state)в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ

в”Ӯ                          2 в”Ӯ AggregateFunction(sum, UInt8) в”Ӯ

в”Ӯ                          1 в”Ӯ AggregateFunction(sum, UInt8) в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

description: Given a state of aggregate function, this function returns the result of aggregation (or finalized state when using a -State combinator).
example: SELECT finalizeAggregation(( SELECT countState(number) FROM numbers(10)));
SELECT finalizeAggregation(( SELECT sumState(number) FROM numbers(10)));

WITH initializeAggregation('sumState', number) AS one_row_sum_state
SELECT
    number,
    finalizeAggregation(one_row_sum_state) AS one_row_sum,
    runningAccumulate(one_row_sum_state) AS cumulative_sum
FROM numbers(10);

в”Ңв”Җnumberв”Җв”¬в”Җone_row_sumв”Җв”¬в”Җcumulative_sumв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ      0 в”Ӯ           0 в”Ӯ              0 в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ      1 в”Ӯ           1 в”Ӯ              1 в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ      2 в”Ӯ           2 в”Ӯ              3 в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ      3 в”Ӯ           3 в”Ӯ              6 в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ      4 в”Ӯ           4 в”Ӯ             10 в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ      5 в”Ӯ           5 в”Ӯ             15 в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ      6 в”Ӯ           6 в”Ӯ             21 в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ      7 в”Ӯ           7 в”Ӯ             28 в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ      8 в”Ӯ           8 в”Ӯ             36 в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ      9 в”Ӯ           9 в”Ӯ             45 в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

description: Accumulates the states of an aggregate function for each row of a data block.
detail:
[DESCRIPTION]: Accumulates the states of an aggregate function for each row of a data block.<sep>[SYNTAX]: runningAccumulate(agg_state[, grouping]);<sep>[ARGUMENTS]: - agg_state вҖ” State of the aggregate function. AggregateFunction .
- grouping вҖ” Grouping key. Optional. The state of the function is reset if the grouping value is changed. It can be any of the supported data types for which the equality operator is defined.
Returned value
- Each resulting row contains a result of the aggregate function, accumulated for all the input rows from 0 to the current position. runningAccumulate resets states for each new data block or when the grouping value changes.
Type depends on the aggregate function used.<sep>[RETURN]: - Each resulting row contains a result of the aggregate function, accumulated for all the input rows from 0 to the current position. runningAccumulate resets states for each new data block or when the grouping value changes.
Type depends on the aggregate function used.<sep>[DEMO]: SELECT k, runningAccumulate(sum_k) AS res FROM (SELECT number AS k, sumState(k) AS sum_k FROM numbers(10) GROUP BY k ORDER BY k); ; в”Ңв”Җkв”Җв”¬в”Җresв”Җв”җ ; SELECT
example: SELECT k, runningAccumulate(sum_k) AS res FROM (SELECT number AS k, sumState(k) AS sum_k FROM numbers(10) GROUP BY k ORDER BY k);

в”Ңв”Җkв”Җв”¬в”Җresв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ 0 в”Ӯ   0 в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ 1 в”Ӯ   1 в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ 2 в”Ӯ   3 в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ 3 в”Ӯ   6 в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ 4 в”Ӯ  10 в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ 5 в”Ӯ  15 в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ 6 в”Ӯ  21 в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ 7 в”Ӯ  28 в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ 8 в”Ӯ  36 в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ 9 в”Ӯ  45 в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

SELECT
    grouping,
    item,
    runningAccumulate(state, grouping) AS res
FROM
(
    SELECT
        toInt8(number / 4) AS grouping,
        number AS item,
        sumState(number) AS state
    FROM numbers(15)
    GROUP BY item
    ORDER BY item ASC
);

description: The function lets you extract data from the table the same way as from a dictionary . Gets the data from Join tables using the specified join key.
detail:
[DESCRIPTION]: The function lets you extract data from the table the same way as from a dictionary . Gets the data from Join tables using the specified join key.<sep>[SYNTAX]: joinGet(join_storage_table_name, `value_column`, join_keys)<sep>[ARGUMENTS]: - join_storage_table_name вҖ” an identifier indicating where the search is performed.
- value_column вҖ” name of the column of the table that contains required data.
- join_keys вҖ” list of keys.
Returned value
- Returns a list of values corresponded to the list of keys.<sep>[RETURN]: - Returns a list of values corresponded to the list of keys.<sep>[DEMO]: SELECT number, joinGet(db_test.id_val, 'val', toUInt32(number)) FROM numbers(4); ; в”Ңв”Җnumberв”Җв”¬в”ҖjoinGet('db_test.id_val', 'val', toUInt32(number))в”Җв”җ ; SELECT number, joinGet(db_test.id_val_nulls, 'val', toUInt32(number)) FROM numbers(4);
example: SELECT number, joinGet(db_test.id_val, 'val', toUInt32(number)) FROM numbers(4);

description: Evaluate an external catboost model. CatBoost is an open-source gradient boosting library developed by Yandex for machine learning. Accepts a path to a catboost model and model arguments (features). Returns Float64.
detail: [DESCRIPTION]: Evaluate an external catboost model. CatBoost is an open-source gradient boosting library developed by Yandex for machine learning. Accepts a path to a catboost model and model arguments (features). Returns Float64.<sep>[SYNTAX]: catboostEvaluate(path_to_model, feature_1, feature_2, ..., feature_n)<sep>[DEMO]: SELECT feat1, ..., feat_n, catboostEvaluate('/path/to/model.bin', feat_1, ..., feat_n) AS prediction
example:
SELECT feat1, ..., feat_n, catboostEvaluate('/path/to/model.bin', feat_1, ..., feat_n) AS prediction
FROM data_table

description: Throw an exception if argument x is true.
detail:
[DESCRIPTION]: Throw an exception if argument x is true.<sep>[SYNTAX]: throwIf(x[, message[, error_code]])<sep>[ARGUMENTS]: - x - the condition to check.
- message - a constant string providing a custom error message. Optional.
- error_code - A constant integer providing a custom error code. Optional.
To use the error_code argument, configuration parameter allow_custom_error_code_in_throwif must be enabled.<sep>[DEMO]: вҶҷ Progress: 0.00 rows, 0.00 B (0.00 rows/s., 0.00 B/s.) Received exception from server (version 19.14.1):
example:
вҶҷ Progress: 0.00 rows, 0.00 B (0.00 rows/s., 0.00 B/s.) Received exception from server (version 19.14.1):
Code: 395. DB::Exception: Received from localhost:9000. DB::Exception: Too many.

description: Returns its argument. Intended for debugging and testing. Allows to cancel using index, and get the query performance of a full scan. When the query is analyzed for possible use of an index, the analyzer ignores everything in identity functions. Also disables constant folding.
detail: [DESCRIPTION]: Returns its argument. Intended for debugging and testing. Allows to cancel using index, and get the query performance of a full scan. When the query is analyzed for possible use of an index, the analyzer ignores everything in identity functions. Also disables constant folding.<sep>[SYNTAX]: identity(x)<sep>[DEMO]: SELECT identity(42); ; в”Ңв”Җidentity(42)в”Җв”җ

description: Returns the current value of a custom setting .
detail:
[DESCRIPTION]: Returns the current value of a custom setting .<sep>[SYNTAX]: getSetting('custom_setting');<sep>[ARGUMENTS]: - custom_setting вҖ” The setting name. String .
Returned value
- The setting's current value.<sep>[RETURN]: - The setting's current value.<sep>[DEMO]: 123

description: Returns the current value of a custom setting or returns the default value specified in the 2nd argument if the custom setting is not set in the current profile.
detail:
[DESCRIPTION]: Returns the current value of a custom setting or returns the default value specified in the 2nd argument if the custom setting is not set in the current profile.<sep>[SYNTAX]: getSettingOrDefault('custom_setting', default_value);<sep>[ARGUMENTS]: - custom_setting вҖ” The setting name. String .
- default_value вҖ” Value to return if custom_setting is not set. Value may be of any data type or Null.
Returned value
- The setting's current value or default_value if setting is not set.<sep>[RETURN]: - The setting's current value or default_value if setting is not set.<sep>[DEMO]: my_value

description: Checks whether the Decimal value is outside its precision or outside the specified precision.
detail:
[DESCRIPTION]: Checks whether the Decimal value is outside its precision or outside the specified precision.<sep>[SYNTAX]: isDecimalOverflow(d, [p])<sep>[ARGUMENTS]: - d вҖ” value. Decimal .
- p вҖ” precision. Optional. If omitted, the initial precision of the first argument is used. This parameter can be helpful to migrate data from/to another database or file. UInt8 .
Returned values
- 1 вҖ” Decimal value has more digits then allowed by its precision,
- 0 вҖ” Decimal value satisfies the specified precision.<sep>[RETURN]: - 1 вҖ” Decimal value has more digits then allowed by its precision,
- 0 вҖ” Decimal value satisfies the specified precision.<sep>[DEMO]: SELECT isDecimalOverflow(toDecimal32(1000000000, 0), 9), ; 1    1    1    1

description: Returns number of decimal digits need to represent a value.
detail:
[DESCRIPTION]: Returns number of decimal digits need to represent a value.<sep>[SYNTAX]: countDigits(x)<sep>[ARGUMENTS]: - x вҖ” Int or Decimal value.
Returned value
- Number of digits. UInt8 .<sep>[RETURN]: - Number of digits. UInt8 .<sep>[DEMO]: SELECT countDigits(toDecimal32(1, 9)), countDigits(toDecimal32(-1, 9)), ; 10    10    19    19    39    39

description: Returns native interface TCP port number listened by this server. If executed in the context of a distributed table, this function generates a normal column with values relevant to each shard. Otherwise it produces a constant value.

Returned value

description:
Returns a list of the current settings profiles for the current user.
The command SET PROFILE could be used to change the current setting profile. If the command SET PROFILE was not used the function returns the profiles specified at the current user's definition (see CREATE USER ).
detail:
[DESCRIPTION]: Returns a list of the current settings profiles for the current user.
The command SET PROFILE could be used to change the current setting profile. If the command SET PROFILE was not used the function returns the profiles specified at the current user's definition (see CREATE USER ).<sep>[SYNTAX]: currentProfiles()<sep>[RETURN]: - List of the current user settings profiles. Array ( String ).

description: Returns settings profiles, assigned to the current user both explicitly and implicitly. Explicitly assigned profiles are the same as returned by the currentProfiles function. Implicitly assigned profiles include parent profiles of other assigned profiles, profiles assigned via granted roles, profiles assigned via their own settings, and the main default profile (see the default_profile section in the main server configuration file).

Array ( String ).

description: Returns all the profiles specified at the current user's definition (see CREATE USER statement).

description: Returns the roles assigned to the current user. The roles can be changed by the SET ROLE statement. If no SET ROLE statement was not, the function currentRoles returns the same as defaultRoles .

description: Returns the roles which are enabled by default for the current user when he logs in. Initially these are all roles granted to the current user (see GRANT ), but that can be changed with the SET DEFAULT ROLE statement.

description: Returns the server port number. When the port is not used by the server, throws an exception.
detail:
[DESCRIPTION]: Returns the server port number. When the port is not used by the server, throws an exception.<sep>[SYNTAX]: getServerPort(port_name)<sep>[ARGUMENTS]: - port_name вҖ” The name of the server port. String . Possible values: 'tcp_port' 'tcp_port_secure' 'http_port' 'https_port' 'interserver_http_port' 'interserver_https_port' 'mysql_port' 'postgresql_port' 'grpc_port' 'prometheus.port'
Returned value
- The number of the server port. UInt16 .<sep>[RETURN]: - The number of the server port. UInt16 .<sep>[DEMO]: SELECT getServerPort('tcp_port'); ; в”Ңв”ҖgetServerPort('tcp_port')в”Җв”җ

See Also

description: Generates random table structure in a format column1_name column1_type, column2_name column2_type, ... .
detail:
[DESCRIPTION]: Generates random table structure in a format column1_name column1_type, column2_name column2_type, ... .<sep>[SYNTAX]: generateRandomStructure([number_of_columns, seed])<sep>[ARGUMENTS]: - number_of_columns вҖ” The desired number of columns in the result table structure. If set to 0 or Null , the number of columns will be random from 1 to 128. Default value: Null .
- seed - Random seed to produce stable results. If seed is not specified or set to Null , it is randomly generated.
All arguments must be constant.
Returned value
- Randomly generated table structure. String .<sep>[RETURN]: - Randomly generated table structure. String .<sep>[DEMO]: SELECT generateRandomStructure() ; в”Ңв”ҖgenerateRandomStructure()в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ ; SELECT generateRandomStructure(1)

description: Converts ClickHouse table structure to CapnProto schema.
detail:
[DESCRIPTION]: Converts ClickHouse table structure to CapnProto schema.<sep>[SYNTAX]: structureToCapnProtoSchema(structure)<sep>[ARGUMENTS]: - structure вҖ” Table structure in a format column1_name column1_type, column2_name column2_type, ... .
- root_struct_name вҖ” Name for root struct in CapnProto schema. Default value - Message ;
Returned value
- CapnProto schema. String .<sep>[RETURN]: - CapnProto schema. String .<sep>[DEMO]: SELECT structureToCapnProtoSchema('column1 String, column2 UInt32, column3 Array(String)') FORMAT RawBLOB ; @0xf96402dd754d0eb7; ; SELECT structureToCapnProtoSchema('column1 Nullable(String), column2 Tuple(element1 UInt32, element2 Array(String)), column3 Map(String, String)') FORMAT RawBLOB
example: SELECT structureToCapnProtoSchema('column1 String, column2 UInt32, column3 Array(String)') FORMAT RawBLOB
SELECT structureToCapnProtoSchema('column1 Nullable(String), column2 Tuple(element1 UInt32, element2 Array(String)), column3 Map(String, String)') FORMAT RawBLOB
SELECT structureToCapnProtoSchema('column1 String, column2 UInt32', 'Root') FORMAT RawBLOB

description: Converts ClickHouse table structure to Protobuf schema.
detail:
[DESCRIPTION]: Converts ClickHouse table structure to Protobuf schema.<sep>[SYNTAX]: structureToProtobufSchema(structure)<sep>[ARGUMENTS]: - structure вҖ” Table structure in a format column1_name column1_type, column2_name column2_type, ... .
- root_message_name вҖ” Name for root message in Protobuf schema. Default value - Message ;
Returned value
- Protobuf schema. String .<sep>[RETURN]: - Protobuf schema. String .<sep>[DEMO]: SELECT structureToProtobufSchema('column1 String, column2 UInt32, column3 Array(String)') FORMAT RawBLOB ; syntax = "proto3"; ; SELECT structureToProtobufSchema('column1 Nullable(String), column2 Tuple(element1 UInt32, element2 Array(String)), column3 Map(String, String)') FORMAT RawBLOB
example: SELECT structureToProtobufSchema('column1 String, column2 UInt32, column3 Array(String)') FORMAT RawBLOB
SELECT structureToProtobufSchema('column1 Nullable(String), column2 Tuple(element1 UInt32, element2 Array(String)), column3 Map(String, String)') FORMAT RawBLOB
SELECT structureToProtobufSchema('column1 String, column2 UInt32', 'Root') FORMAT RawBLOB

description:
Returns a formatted, possibly multi-line, version of the given SQL query.
Throws an exception if the query is not well-formed. To return NULL instead, function formatQueryOrNull() may be used.
detail:
[DESCRIPTION]: Returns a formatted, possibly multi-line, version of the given SQL query.
Throws an exception if the query is not well-formed. To return NULL instead, function formatQueryOrNull() may be used.<sep>[SYNTAX]: formatQuery(query)
formatQueryOrNull(query)<sep>[ARGUMENTS]: - query - The SQL query to be formatted. String
Returned value
- The formatted query. String .<sep>[RETURN]: - The formatted query. String .<sep>[DEMO]: в”Ңв”ҖformatQuery('select a,    b FRom tab WHERE a > 3 and  b < 3')в”Җв”җ
example:
в”Ңв”ҖformatQuery('select a,    b FRom tab WHERE a > 3 and  b < 3')в”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ SELECT
    a,
    b
FROM tab
WHERE (a > 3) AND (b < 3)            в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

description:
Like formatQuery() but the returned formatted string contains no line breaks.
Throws an exception if the query is not well-formed. To return NULL instead, function formatQuerySingleLineOrNull() may be used.
detail:
[DESCRIPTION]: Like formatQuery() but the returned formatted string contains no line breaks.
Throws an exception if the query is not well-formed. To return NULL instead, function formatQuerySingleLineOrNull() may be used.<sep>[SYNTAX]: formatQuerySingleLine(query)
formatQuerySingleLineOrNull(query)<sep>[ARGUMENTS]: - query - The SQL query to be formatted. String
Returned value
- The formatted query. String .<sep>[RETURN]: - The formatted query. String .<sep>[DEMO]: в”Ңв”ҖformatQuerySingleLine('select a,    b FRom tab WHERE a > 3 and  b < 3')в”Җв”җ
example:
в”Ңв”ҖformatQuerySingleLine('select a,    b FRom tab WHERE a > 3 and  b < 3')в”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ SELECT a, b FROM tab WHERE (a > 3) AND (b < 3)                          в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

description: Extracts a column with specified type from a Variant column.
detail:
[DESCRIPTION]: Extracts a column with specified type from a Variant column.<sep>[SYNTAX]: variantElement(variant, type_name, [, default_value])<sep>[ARGUMENTS]: - variant вҖ” Variant column. Variant .
- type_name вҖ” The name of the variant type to extract. String .
- default_value - The default value that will be used if variant doesn't have variant with specified type. Can be any type. Optional.
Returned value
- Subcolumn of a Variant column with specified type.<sep>[RETURN]: - Subcolumn of a Variant column with specified type.<sep>[DEMO]: в”Ңв”Җvв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”ҖvariantElement(v, 'String')в”Җв”¬в”ҖvariantElement(v, 'UInt64')в”Җв”¬в”ҖvariantElement(v, 'Array(UInt64)')в”Җв”җ
example:

description: Calculates minimum required sample size for an A/B test comparing conversions (proportions) in two samples.
detail:
[DESCRIPTION]: Calculates minimum required sample size for an A/B test comparing conversions (proportions) in two samples.<sep>[SYNTAX]: minSampleSizeConversion(baseline, mde, power, alpha)<sep>[ARGUMENTS]: - baseline вҖ” Baseline conversion. Float .
- mde вҖ” Minimum detectable effect (MDE) as percentage points (e.g. for a baseline conversion 0.25 the MDE 0.03 means an expected change to 0.25 Вұ 0.03). Float .
- power вҖ” Required statistical power of a test (1 - probability of Type II error). Float .
- alpha вҖ” Required significance level of a test (probability of Type I error). Float .
Returned value
A named Tuple with 3 elements:
- "minimum_sample_size" вҖ” Required sample size. Float64 .
- "detect_range_lower" вҖ” Lower bound of the range of values not detectable with the returned required sample size (i.e. all values less than or equal to "detect_range_lower" are detectable with the provided alpha and power ). Calculated as baseline - mde . Float64 .
- "detect_range_upper" вҖ” Upper bound of the range of values not detectable with the returned required sample size (i.e. all values greater than or equal to "detect_range_upper" are detectable with the provided alpha and power ). Calculated as baseline + mde . Float64 .<sep>[RETURN]: A named Tuple with 3 elements:
- "minimum_sample_size" вҖ” Required sample size. Float64 .
- "detect_range_lower" вҖ” Lower bound of the range of values not detectable with the returned required sample size (i.e. all values less than or equal to "detect_range_lower" are detectable with the provided alpha and power ). Calculated as baseline - mde . Float64 .
- "detect_range_upper" вҖ” Upper bound of the range of values not detectable with the returned required sample size (i.e. all values greater than or equal to "detect_range_upper" are detectable with the provided alpha and power ). Calculated as baseline + mde . Float64 .<sep>[DEMO]: в”Ңв”Җsample_sizeв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ

description: Calculates minimum required sample size for an A/B test comparing means of a continuous metric in two samples.
detail:
[DESCRIPTION]: Calculates minimum required sample size for an A/B test comparing means of a continuous metric in two samples.<sep>[SYNTAX]: minSampleSizeContinous(baseline, sigma, mde, power, alpha)<sep>[ARGUMENTS]: - baseline вҖ” Baseline value of a metric. Integer or Float .
- sigma вҖ” Baseline standard deviation of a metric. Integer or Float .
- mde вҖ” Minimum detectable effect (MDE) as percentage of the baseline value (e.g. for a baseline value 112.25 the MDE 0.03 means an expected change to 112.25 Вұ 112.25*0.03). Integer or Float .
- power вҖ” Required statistical power of a test (1 - probability of Type II error). Integer or Float .
- alpha вҖ” Required significance level of a test (probability of Type I error). Integer or Float .
Returned value
A named Tuple with 3 elements:
- "minimum_sample_size" вҖ” Required sample size. Float64 .
- "detect_range_lower" вҖ” Lower bound of the range of values not detectable with the returned required sample size (i.e. all values less than or equal to "detect_range_lower" are detectable with the provided alpha and power ). Calculated as baseline * (1 - mde) . Float64 .
- "detect_range_upper" вҖ” Upper bound of the range of values not detectable with the returned required sample size (i.e. all values greater than or equal to "detect_range_upper" are detectable with the provided alpha and power ). Calculated as baseline * (1 + mde) . Float64 .<sep>[RETURN]: A named Tuple with 3 elements:
- "minimum_sample_size" вҖ” Required sample size. Float64 .
- "detect_range_lower" вҖ” Lower bound of the range of values not detectable with the returned required sample size (i.e. all values less than or equal to "detect_range_lower" are detectable with the provided alpha and power ). Calculated as baseline * (1 - mde) . Float64 .
- "detect_range_upper" вҖ” Upper bound of the range of values not detectable with the returned required sample size (i.e. all values greater than or equal to "detect_range_upper" are detectable with the provided alpha and power ). Calculated as baseline * (1 + mde) . Float64 .<sep>[DEMO]: в”Ңв”Җsample_sizeв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ

description: Retrieves the connection ID of the client that submitted the current query and returns it as a UInt64 integer.
detail:
[DESCRIPTION]: Retrieves the connection ID of the client that submitted the current query and returns it as a UInt64 integer.<sep>[SYNTAX]: connectionId()<sep>[ARGUMENTS]: None.
Returned value
The current connection ID. UInt64 .
Implementation details
This function is most useful in debugging scenarios or for internal purposes within the MySQL handler. It was created for compatibility with MySQL's CONNECTION_ID function It is not typically used in production queries.<sep>[RETURN]: The current connection ID. UInt64 .
Implementation details
This function is most useful in debugging scenarios or for internal purposes within the MySQL handler. It was created for compatibility with MySQL's CONNECTION_ID function It is not typically used in production queries.<sep>[DEMO]: SELECT connectionId();

description:
Get the value of an HTTP header.
If there is no such header or the current request is not performed via the HTTP interface, the function returns an empty string. Certain HTTP headers (e.g., Authentication and X-ClickHouse-* ) are restricted.
The function requires the setting allow_get_client_http_header to be enabled. The setting is not enabled by default for security reasons, because some headers, such as Cookie , could contain sensitive info.
HTTP headers are case sensitive for this function.
If the function is used in the context of a distributed query, it returns non-empty result only on the initiator node.

HTTP headers are case sensitive for this function.
If the function is used in the context of a distributed query, it returns non-empty result only on the initiator node.

description: Returns the ID of a transaction .
detail:
[DESCRIPTION]: Returns the ID of a transaction .<sep>[SYNTAX]: transactionID()<sep>[RETURN]: - Returns a tuple consisting of start_csn , local_tid and host_id . Tuple .
- start_csn : Global sequential number, the newest commit timestamp that was seen when this transaction began. UInt64 .
- local_tid : Local sequential number that is unique for each transaction started by this host within a specific start_csn. UInt64 .
- host_id : UUID of the host that has started this transaction. UUID .<sep>[DEMO]: BEGIN TRANSACTION; ; в”Ңв”ҖtransactionID()в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ

description: Takes a constant string argument and returns the value of the global variable with that name. This function is intended for compatibility with MySQL and not needed or useful for normal operation of ClickHouse. Only few dummy global variables are defined.
detail:
[DESCRIPTION]: Takes a constant string argument and returns the value of the global variable with that name. This function is intended for compatibility with MySQL and not needed or useful for normal operation of ClickHouse. Only few dummy global variables are defined.<sep>[SYNTAX]: globalVariable(name)<sep>[ARGUMENTS]: - name вҖ” Global variable name. String .
Returned value
- Returns the value of variable name .<sep>[RETURN]: - Returns the value of variable name .<sep>[DEMO]: SELECT globalVariable('max_allowed_packet'); ; в”Ңв”ҖglobalVariable('max_allowed_packet')в”Җв”җ

description: In contrast to standard SQL, ClickHouse has strong typing. In other words, it does not make implicit conversions between types. Each function works for a specific set of types. This means that sometimes you need to use type conversion functions.
detail: [DESCRIPTION]: In contrast to standard SQL, ClickHouse has strong typing. In other words, it does not make implicit conversions between types. Each function works for a specific set of types. This means that sometimes you need to use type conversion functions.

description: All expressions in a query that have the same AST (the same record or same result of syntactic parsing) are considered to have identical values. Such expressions are concatenated and executed once. Identical subqueries are also eliminated this way.
detail: [DESCRIPTION]: All expressions in a query that have the same AST (the same record or same result of syntactic parsing) are considered to have identical values. Such expressions are concatenated and executed once. Identical subqueries are also eliminated this way.

description: All functions return a single value as the result (not several values, and not zero values). The type of result is usually defined only by the types of arguments, not by the values. Exceptions are the tupleElement function (the a.N operator), and the toFixedString function.
detail: [DESCRIPTION]: All functions return a single value as the result (not several values, and not zero values). The type of result is usually defined only by the types of arguments, not by the values. Exceptions are the tupleElement function (the a.N operator), and the toFixedString function.

description:
For simplicity, certain functions can only work with constants for some arguments. For example, the right argument of the LIKE operator must be a constant. Almost all functions return a constant for constant arguments. The exception is functions that generate random numbers. The 'now' function returns different values for queries that were run at different times, but the result is considered a constant, since constancy is only important within a single query. A constant expression is also considered a constant (for example, the right half of the LIKE operator can be constructed from multiple constants).
Functions can be implemented in different ways for constant and non-constant arguments (different code is executed). But the results for a constant and for a true column containing only the same value should match each other.
detail:
[DESCRIPTION]: For simplicity, certain functions can only work with constants for some arguments. For example, the right argument of the LIKE operator must be a constant. Almost all functions return a constant for constant arguments. The exception is functions that generate random numbers. The 'now' function returns different values for queries that were run at different times, but the result is considered a constant, since constancy is only important within a single query. A constant expression is also considered a constant (for example, the right half of the LIKE operator can be constructed from multiple constants).
Functions can be implemented in different ways for constant and non-constant arguments (different code is executed). But the results for a constant and for a true column containing only the same value should match each other.

description:
Functions have the following behaviors:
- If at least one of the arguments of the function is NULL , the function result is also NULL .
- Special behavior that is specified individually in the description of each function. In the ClickHouse source code, these functions have UseDefaultImplementationForNulls=false .
detail:
[DESCRIPTION]: Functions have the following behaviors:
- If at least one of the arguments of the function is NULL , the function result is also NULL .
- Special behavior that is specified individually in the description of each function. In the ClickHouse source code, these functions have UseDefaultImplementationForNulls=false .

description: Functions can't change the values of their arguments вҖ“ any changes are returned as the result. Thus, the result of calculating separate functions does not depend on the order in which the functions are written in the query.
detail: [DESCRIPTION]: Functions can't change the values of their arguments вҖ“ any changes are returned as the result. Thus, the result of calculating separate functions does not depend on the order in which the functions are written in the query.

description: Higher-order functions can only accept lambda functions as their functional argument. To pass a lambda function to a higher-order function use -> operator. The left side of the arrow has a formal parameter, which is any ID, or multiple formal parameters вҖ“ any IDs in a tuple. The right side of the arrow has an expression that can use these formal parameters, as well as any table columns.
detail: [DESCRIPTION]: Higher-order functions can only accept lambda functions as their functional argument. To pass a lambda function to a higher-order function use -> operator. The left side of the arrow has a formal parameter, which is any ID, or multiple formal parameters вҖ“ any IDs in a tuple. The right side of the arrow has an expression that can use these formal parameters, as well as any table columns.

description: ClickHouse supports user-defined functions. See UDFs .
detail: [DESCRIPTION]: ClickHouse supports user-defined functions. See UDFs .

description:
Returns a random UInt32 number with uniform distribution.
Uses a linear congruential generator with an initial state obtained from the system, which means that while it appears random, it's not truly random and can be predictable if the initial state is known. For scenarios where true randomness is crucial, consider using alternative methods like system-level calls or integrating with external libraries.
detail:
[DESCRIPTION]: Returns a random UInt32 number with uniform distribution.
Uses a linear congruential generator with an initial state obtained from the system, which means that while it appears random, it's not truly random and can be predictable if the initial state is known. For scenarios where true randomness is crucial, consider using alternative methods like system-level calls or integrating with external libraries.<sep>[SYNTAX]: rand()<sep>[ARGUMENTS]: None.
Returned value
Returns a number of type UInt32.<sep>[RETURN]: Returns a number of type UInt32.<sep>[DEMO]: SELECT rand();

description: Returns a random UInt64 integer (UInt64) number
detail:
[DESCRIPTION]: Returns a random UInt64 integer (UInt64) number<sep>[SYNTAX]: rand64()<sep>[ARGUMENTS]: Returns a number UInt64 number with uniform distribution.
Uses a linear congruential generator with an initial state obtained from the system, which means that while it appears random, it's not truly random and can be predictable if the initial state is known. For scenarios where true randomness is crucial, consider using alternative methods like system-level calls or integrating with external libraries.<sep>[DEMO]: SELECT rand64();

description: Returns a random Float64 number.
detail: [DESCRIPTION]: Returns a random Float64 number.<sep>[SYNTAX]: randCanonical()<sep>[ARGUMENTS]: Returns a Float64 value between 0 (inclusive) and 1 (exclusive).<sep>[DEMO]: SELECT randCanonical();

description: Generates a single constant column filled with a random value. Unlike rand , this function ensures the same random value appears in every row of the generated column, making it useful for scenarios requiring a consistent random seed across rows in a single query.
Returns a random Float64 drawn uniformly from interval [ min , max ].
detail:
[DESCRIPTION]: Returns a random Float64 drawn uniformly from interval [ min , max ].<sep>[SYNTAX]: randUniform(min, max)<sep>[ARGUMENTS]: - min - Float64 - left boundary of the range,
- max - Float64 - right boundary of the range.
Returned value
A random number of type Float64 .<sep>[RETURN]: A random number of type Float64 .<sep>[DEMO]: SELECT randUniform(5.5, 10) FROM numbers(5) ; в”Ңв”ҖrandUniform(5.5, 10)в”Җв”җ

[SYNTAX]: fuzzBits(s, prob)<sep>[ARGUMENTS]: - s - String or FixedString ,
- prob - constant Float32/64 between 0.0 and 1.0.
Returned value
Fuzzed string with same type as s .<sep>[RETURN]: Fuzzed string with same type as s .<sep>[DEMO]: в”Ңв”ҖfuzzBits(materialize('abacaba'), 0.1)в”Җв”җ
example:
в”Ңв”ҖfuzzBits(materialize('abacaba'), 0.1)в”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ abaaaja                               в”Ӯ

description:
Returns the largest rounded number less than or equal x . A rounded number is a multiple of 1 / 10 * N, or the nearest number of the appropriate data type if 1 / 10 * N isn't exact.
Integer arguments may be rounded with negative N argument, with non-negative N the function returns x , i.e. does nothing.
If rounding causes an overflow (for example, floor(-128, -1) ), the result is undefined.
detail:
[DESCRIPTION]: Returns the largest rounded number less than or equal x . A rounded number is a multiple of 1 / 10 * N, or the nearest number of the appropriate data type if 1 / 10 * N isn't exact.
Integer arguments may be rounded with negative N argument, with non-negative N the function returns x , i.e. does nothing.
If rounding causes an overflow (for example, floor(-128, -1) ), the result is undefined.<sep>[SYNTAX]: floor(x[, N])<sep>[ARGUMENTS]: - x - The value to round. Float* , Decimal* , or (U)Int* .
- N . (U)Int* . The default is zero, which means rounding to an integer. Can be negative.
Returned value
A rounded number of the same type as x .<sep>[RETURN]: A rounded number of the same type as x .<sep>[DEMO]: SELECT floor(123.45, 1) AS rounded ; в”Ңв”Җroundedв”Җв”җ ; SELECT floor(123.45, -1)

description: Like floor but returns the smallest rounded number greater than or equal x .
detail: [DESCRIPTION]: Like floor but returns the smallest rounded number greater than or equal x .<sep>[SYNTAX]: ceiling(x[, N])

description: Like floor but returns the rounded number with largest absolute value that has an absolute value less than or equal to x 's.
detail: [DESCRIPTION]: Like floor but returns the rounded number with largest absolute value that has an absolute value less than or equal to x 's.<sep>[SYNTAX]: truncate(x[, N])<sep>[DEMO]: SELECT truncate(123.499, 1) AS res;

description:
Rounds a value to a specified number of decimal places.
The function returns the nearest number of the specified order. If the input value has equal distance to two neighboring numbers, the function uses banker's rounding for Float* inputs and rounds away from zero for the other number types ( Decimal* .
detail:
[DESCRIPTION]: Rounds a value to a specified number of decimal places.
The function returns the nearest number of the specified order. If the input value has equal distance to two neighboring numbers, the function uses banker's rounding for Float* inputs and rounds away from zero for the other number types ( Decimal* .<sep>[SYNTAX]: round(x[, N])<sep>[ARGUMENTS]: - x вҖ” A number to round. Float* , Decimal* , or (U)Int* .
- N вҖ” The number of decimal places to round to. Integer. Defaults to 0 . If N > 0 , the function rounds to the right of the decimal point. If N < 0 , the function rounds to the left of the decimal point. If N = 0 , the function rounds to the next integer.
Returned value:
A rounded number of the same type as x .<sep>[RETURN]: A rounded number of the same type as x .<sep>[DEMO]: SELECT number / 2 AS x, round(x) FROM system.numbers LIMIT 3; ; в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җxв”Җв”¬в”Җround(divide(number, 2))в”Җв”җ ; SELECT cast(number / 2 AS  Decimal(10,4)) AS x, round(x) FROM system.numbers LIMIT 3;

description:
Rounds a number to a specified decimal position.
If the rounding number is halfway between two numbers, the function uses banker's rounding. Banker's rounding is a method of rounding fractional numbers When the rounding number is halfway between two numbers, it's rounded to the nearest even digit at the specified decimal position. For example: 3.5 rounds up to 4, 2.5 rounds down to 2. It's the default rounding method for floating point numbers defined in IEEE 754 . The round function performs the same rounding for floating point numbers. The roundBankers function also rounds integers the same way, for example, roundBankers(45, -1) = 40 .
In other cases, the function rounds numbers to the nearest integer.
Using banker's rounding, you can reduce the effect that rounding numbers has on the results of summing or subtracting these numbers.
For example, sum numbers 1.5, 2.5, 3.5, 4.5 with different rounding:
- No rounding: 1.5 + 2.5 + 3.5 + 4.5 = 12.
- Banker's rounding: 2 + 2 + 4 + 4 = 12.
- Rounding to the nearest integer: 2 + 3 + 4 + 5 = 14.
detail:
[DESCRIPTION]: Rounds a number to a specified decimal position.
If the rounding number is halfway between two numbers, the function uses banker's rounding. Banker's rounding is a method of rounding fractional numbers When the rounding number is halfway between two numbers, it's rounded to the nearest even digit at the specified decimal position. For example: 3.5 rounds up to 4, 2.5 rounds down to 2. It's the default rounding method for floating point numbers defined in IEEE 754 . The round function performs the same rounding for floating point numbers. The roundBankers function also rounds integers the same way, for example, roundBankers(45, -1) = 40 .
In other cases, the function rounds numbers to the nearest integer.
Using banker's rounding, you can reduce the effect that rounding numbers has on the results of summing or subtracting these numbers.
For example, sum numbers 1.5, 2.5, 3.5, 4.5 with different rounding:
- No rounding: 1.5 + 2.5 + 3.5 + 4.5 = 12.
- Banker's rounding: 2 + 2 + 4 + 4 = 12.
- Rounding to the nearest integer: 2 + 3 + 4 + 5 = 14.<sep>[SYNTAX]: roundBankers(x [, N])<sep>[ARGUMENTS]: - N > 0 вҖ” The function rounds the number to the given position right of the decimal point. Example: roundBankers(3.55, 1) = 3.6 .
- N < 0 вҖ” The function rounds the number to the given position left of the decimal point. Example: roundBankers(24.55, -1) = 20 .
- N = 0 вҖ” The function rounds the number to an integer. In this case the argument can be omitted. Example: roundBankers(2.5) = 2 .
- x вҖ” A number to round. Float* , Decimal* , or (U)Int* .
- N вҖ” The number of decimal places to round to. Integer. Defaults to 0 . If N > 0 , the function rounds to the right of the decimal point. If N < 0 , the function rounds to the left of the decimal point. If N = 0 , the function rounds to the next integer.
Returned value
A value rounded by the banker's rounding method.<sep>[RETURN]: A value rounded by the banker's rounding method.<sep>[DEMO]: SELECT number / 2 AS x, roundBankers(x, 0) AS b FROM system.numbers LIMIT 10 ; в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җxв”Җв”¬в”Җbв”Җв”җ
example: SELECT number / 2 AS x, roundBankers(x, 0) AS b FROM system.numbers LIMIT 10

в”Ӯ   1 в”Ӯ 1 в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ 1.5 в”Ӯ 2 в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ   2 в”Ӯ 2 в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ 2.5 в”Ӯ 2 в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ   3 в”Ӯ 3 в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ 3.5 в”Ӯ 4 в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ   4 в”Ӯ 4 в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ 4.5 в”Ӯ 4 в”Ӯ

description: Accepts a number. If the number is less than one, it returns 0 . Otherwise, it rounds the number down to the nearest (whole non-negative) degree of two.
detail:
[DESCRIPTION]: Accepts a number. If the number is less than one, it returns 0 . Otherwise, it rounds the number down to the nearest (whole non-negative) degree of two.<sep>[SYNTAX]: roundToExp2(num)<sep>[ARGUMENTS]: - num : A number to round. UInt / Float .
Returned value
- 0 , for num < 1 \lt 1 < 1 . UInt8 .
- num rounded down to the nearest (whole non-negative) degree of two. UInt / Float equivalent to the input type.<sep>[RETURN]: - 0 , for num < 1 \lt 1 < 1 . UInt8 .
- num rounded down to the nearest (whole non-negative) degree of two. UInt / Float equivalent to the input type.<sep>[DEMO]: SELECT *, roundToExp2(*) FROM system.numbers WHERE number IN (0, 2, 5, 10, 19, 50) ; в”Ңв”Җnumberв”Җв”¬в”ҖroundToExp2(number)в”Җв”җ

detail:

Otherwise, it rounds the number down to numbers from the set of commonly used durations: 1, 10, 30, 60, 120, 180, 240, 300, 600, 1200, 1800, 3600, 7200, 18000, 36000 .<sep>[SYNTAX]: roundDuration(num)<sep>[ARGUMENTS]: - num : A number to round to one of the numbers in the set of common durations. description: Accepts a number within various commonly used ranges of human age and returns either a maximum or a minimum within that range.
detail:
[DESCRIPTION]: Accepts a number within various commonly used ranges of human age and returns either a maximum or a minimum within that range.<sep>[SYNTAX]: roundAge(num)<sep>[ARGUMENTS]: - age : A number representing an age in years. UInt / Float .
Returned value
- Returns 0 , for a g e < 1 age \lt 1 a g e < 1 .

description: Accepts a number and rounds it down to an element in the specified array. If the value is less than the lowest bound, the lowest bound is returned.
detail:
[DESCRIPTION]: Accepts a number and rounds it down to an element in the specified array. If the value is less than the lowest bound, the lowest bound is returned.<sep>[SYNTAX]: roundDown(num, arr)<sep>[ARGUMENTS]: - num : A number to round down. Numeric .
- arr : Array of elements to round age down to. Array of UInt / Float type.
Returned value
- Number rounded down to an element in arr . If the value is less than the lowest bound, the lowest bound is returned. UInt / Float type deduced from the type of arr .<sep>[RETURN]: - Number rounded down to an element in arr . If the value is less than the lowest bound, the lowest bound is returned. description: Splits a string into substrings separated by a specified character. Uses a constant string separator which consists of exactly one character. Returns an array of selected substrings. Empty substrings may be selected if the separator occurs at the beginning or end of the string, or if there are multiple consecutive separators.
detail:
[DESCRIPTION]: Splits a string into substrings separated by a specified character. Uses a constant string separator which consists of exactly one character. Returns an array of selected substrings. Empty substrings may be selected if the separator occurs at the beginning or end of the string, or if there are multiple consecutive separators.<sep>[SYNTAX]: splitByChar(separator, s[, max_substrings]))<sep>[ARGUMENTS]: - separator вҖ” The separator must be a single-byte character. String .
- s вҖ” The string to split. String .
- max_substrings вҖ” An optional Int64 defaulting to 0. If max_substrings > 0, the returned array will contain at most max_substrings substrings, otherwise the function will return as many substrings as possible.
Returned value(s)
- An array of selected substrings. Array ( String ).
Empty substrings may be selected when:
- A separator occurs at the beginning or end of the string;
- There are multiple consecutive separators;
- The original string s is empty.<sep>[DEMO]: в”Ңв”ҖsplitByChar(',', '1,2,3,abcde')в”Җв”җ

description: Splits a string into substrings separated by a string. It uses a constant string separator of multiple characters as the separator. If the string separator is empty, it will split the string s into an array of single characters.
detail:
[DESCRIPTION]: Splits a string into substrings separated by a string. It uses a constant string separator of multiple characters as the separator. If the string separator is empty, it will split the string s into an array of single characters.<sep>[SYNTAX]: splitByString(separator, s[, max_substrings]))<sep>[ARGUMENTS]: - separator вҖ” The separator. String .
- s вҖ” The string to split. String .
- max_substrings вҖ” An optional Int64 defaulting to 0. When max_substrings > 0, the returned substrings will be no more than max_substrings , otherwise the function will return as many substrings as possible.
Returned value(s)
- An array of selected substrings. Array ( String ).
Empty substrings may be selected when:
- A non-empty separator occurs at the beginning or end of the string;
- There are multiple consecutive non-empty separators;
- The original string s is empty while the separator is not empty.<sep>[DEMO]: в”Ңв”ҖsplitByString(', ', '1, 2 3, 4,5, abcde')в”Җв”җ ; в”Ңв”ҖsplitByString('', 'abcde')в”Җв”җ

description: Splits a string into substrings separated by a regular expression. It uses a regular expression string regexp as the separator. If the regexp is empty, it will split the string s into an array of single characters. If no match is found for this regular expression, the string s won't be split.
detail:
[DESCRIPTION]: Splits a string into substrings separated by a regular expression. It uses a regular expression string regexp as the separator. If the regexp is empty, it will split the string s into an array of single characters. If no match is found for this regular expression, the string s won't be split.<sep>[SYNTAX]: splitByRegexp(regexp, s[, max_substrings]))<sep>[ARGUMENTS]: - regexp вҖ” Regular expression. Constant. String or FixedString .
- s вҖ” The string to split. String .
- max_substrings вҖ” An optional Int64 defaulting to 0. When max_substrings > 0, the returned substrings will be no more than max_substrings , otherwise the function will return as many substrings as possible.
Returned value(s)
- An array of selected substrings. Array ( String ).
Empty substrings may be selected when:
- A non-empty regular expression match occurs at the beginning or end of the string;
- There are multiple consecutive non-empty regular expression matches;
- The original string s is empty while the regular expression is not empty.<sep>[DEMO]: в”Ңв”ҖsplitByRegexp('\\d+', 'a12bc23de345f')в”Җв”җ ; в”Ңв”ҖsplitByRegexp('', 'abcde')в”Җв”җ

description: Splits a string into substrings separated by whitespace characters. Returns an array of selected substrings.
detail:
[DESCRIPTION]: Splits a string into substrings separated by whitespace characters. Returns an array of selected substrings.<sep>[SYNTAX]: splitByWhitespace(s[, max_substrings]))<sep>[ARGUMENTS]: - s вҖ” The string to split. String .
- max_substrings вҖ” An optional Int64 defaulting to 0. When max_substrings > 0, the returned substrings will be no more than max_substrings , otherwise the function will return as many substrings as possible.
Returned value(s)
- An array of selected substrings. Array ( String ).<sep>[DEMO]: в”Ңв”ҖsplitByWhitespace('  1!  a,  b.  ')в”Җв”җ

description: Splits a string into substrings separated by whitespace and punctuation characters. Returns an array of selected substrings.
detail:
[DESCRIPTION]: Splits a string into substrings separated by whitespace and punctuation characters. Returns an array of selected substrings.<sep>[SYNTAX]: splitByNonAlpha(s[, max_substrings]))<sep>[ARGUMENTS]: - s вҖ” The string to split. String .
- max_substrings вҖ” An optional Int64 defaulting to 0. When max_substrings > 0, the returned substrings will be no more than max_substrings , otherwise the function will return as many substrings as possible.
Returned value(s)
- An array of selected substrings. Array ( String ).<sep>[DEMO]: SELECT splitByNonAlpha('  1!  a,  b.  '); ; в”Ңв”ҖsplitByNonAlpha('  1!  a,  b.  ')в”Җв”җ

description: Concatenates string representations of values listed in the array with the separator. separator is an optional parameter: a constant string, set to an empty string by default. Returns the string.
detail: [DESCRIPTION]: Concatenates string representations of values listed in the array with the separator. separator is an optional parameter: a constant string, set to an empty string by default. Returns the string.<sep>[SYNTAX]: arrayStringConcat(arr\[, separator\])<sep>[DEMO]: в”Ңв”ҖDateStringв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ

description: Selects substrings of consecutive bytes from the ranges a-z and A-Z.Returns an array of substrings.
detail:
[DESCRIPTION]: Selects substrings of consecutive bytes from the ranges a-z and A-Z.Returns an array of substrings.<sep>[SYNTAX]: alphaTokens(s[, max_substrings]))<sep>[ARGUMENTS]: - s вҖ” The string to split. String .
- max_substrings вҖ” An optional Int64 defaulting to 0. When max_substrings > 0, the returned substrings will be no more than max_substrings , otherwise the function will return as many substrings as possible.
Returned value(s)
- An array of selected substrings. Array ( String ).<sep>[DEMO]: SELECT alphaTokens('abca1abc'); ; в”Ңв”ҖalphaTokens('abca1abc')в”Җв”җ

description: Extracts all groups from non-overlapping substrings matched by a regular expression.
detail:
[DESCRIPTION]: Extracts all groups from non-overlapping substrings matched by a regular expression.<sep>[SYNTAX]: extractAllGroups(text, regexp)<sep>[ARGUMENTS]: - text вҖ” String or FixedString .
- regexp вҖ” Regular expression. Constant. String or FixedString .
Returned values
- If the function finds at least one matching group, it returns Array(Array(String)) column, clustered by group_id (1 to N, where N is number of capturing groups in regexp ). If there is no matching group, it returns an empty array. Array .<sep>[RETURN]: - If the function finds at least one matching group, it returns Array(Array(String)) column, clustered by group_id (1 to N, where N is number of capturing groups in regexp ). If there is no matching group, it returns an empty array. Array .<sep>[DEMO]: в”Ңв”ҖextractAllGroups('abc=123, 8="hkl"', '("[^"]+"|\\w+)=("[^"]+"|\\w+)')в”Җв”җ

description: Splits a UTF-8 string into n-grams of ngramsize symbols.
detail:

Returned values

description: Splits a string into tokens using the given tokenizer. The default tokenizer uses non-alphanumeric ASCII characters as separators.
detail:
[DESCRIPTION]: Splits a string into tokens using the given tokenizer. The default tokenizer uses non-alphanumeric ASCII characters as separators.<sep>[ARGUMENTS]: - value вҖ” The input string. String or FixedString .
- tokenizer вҖ” The tokenizer to use. Valid arguments are default , ngram , split , and no_op . Optional, if not set explicitly, defaults to default . const String
- ngrams вҖ” Only relevant if argument tokenizer is ngram : An optional parameter which defines the length of the ngrams. If not set explicitly, defaults to 3 . UInt8 .
- separators вҖ” Only relevant if argument tokenizer is split : An optional parameter which defines the separator strings. If not set explicitly, defaults to [' '] . Array(String) .
Returned value
- The resulting array of tokens from input string. Array .<sep>[RETURN]: - The resulting array of tokens from input string. Array .<sep>[DEMO]: в”Ңв”Җtokensв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ ; в”Ңв”Җtokensв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ

description:
Checks whether the input string is empty. A string is considered non-empty if it contains at least one byte, even if this byte is a space or the null byte.
The function is also available for arrays and UUIDs .
detail:

description:

description:

Alias: OCTET_LENGTH
detail:

Alias: OCTET_LENGTH<sep>[SYNTAX]: length(s)<sep>[ARGUMENTS]: - s вҖ” An input string or array. String / Array .

description: Returns the length of a string in Unicode code points rather than in bytes or characters. It assumes that the string contains valid UTF-8 encoded text. If this assumption is violated, no exception is thrown and the result is undefined.
detail:

If this assumption is violated, no exception is thrown and the result is undefined.<sep>[SYNTAX]: lengthUTF8(s)<sep>[ARGUMENTS]: - s вҖ” String containing valid UTF-8 encoded text. String .

description: Returns a substring of string s with a specified offset starting from the left.
detail:
[DESCRIPTION]: Returns a substring of string s with a specified offset starting from the left.<sep>[SYNTAX]: left(s, offset)<sep>[ARGUMENTS]: - s вҖ” The string to calculate a substring from. String or FixedString .
- offset вҖ” The number of bytes of the offset. (U)Int* .
Returned value
- For positive offset : A substring of s with offset many bytes, starting from the left of the string.
- For negative offset : A substring of s with length(s) - |offset| bytes, starting from the left of the string.
- An empty string if length is 0.<sep>[RETURN]: - For positive offset : A substring of s with offset many bytes, starting from the left of the string.
- For negative offset : A substring of s with length(s) - |offset| bytes, starting from the left of the string.
- An empty string if length is 0.<sep>[DEMO]: SELECT left('Hello', 3); ; Hel ; SELECT left('Hello', -3);

- An empty string if length is 0.<sep>[DEMO]: SELECT leftUTF8('РҹСҖРёРІРөСӮ', 4); ; РҹСҖРёРІ ; SELECT leftUTF8('РҹСҖРёРІРөСӮ', -4);

description: Pads a string from the left with spaces or with a specified string (multiple times, if needed) until the resulting string reaches the specified length .
detail:
[DESCRIPTION]: Pads a string from the left with spaces or with a specified string (multiple times, if needed) until the resulting string reaches the specified length .<sep>[SYNTAX]: leftPad(string, length[, pad_string])<sep>[ARGUMENTS]: - string вҖ” Input string that should be padded. String .
- length вҖ” The length of the resulting string. UInt or Int . If the value is smaller than the input string length, then the input string is shortened to length characters.
- pad_string вҖ” The string to pad the input string with. String . Optional. If not specified, then the input string is padded with spaces.
Returned value
- A left-padded string of the given length. String .<sep>[RETURN]: - A left-padded string of the given length. String .<sep>[DEMO]: в”Ңв”ҖleftPad('abc', 7, '*')в”Җв”¬в”ҖleftPad('def', 7)в”Җв”җ

description: Compare two strings lexicographically.
detail:
[DESCRIPTION]: Compare two strings lexicographically.<sep>[SYNTAX]: compareSubstrings(string1, string2, string1_offset, string2_offset, num_bytes);<sep>[ARGUMENTS]: - string1 вҖ” The first string to compare. String
- string2 - The second string to compare. String
- string1_offset вҖ” The position (zero-based) in string1 from which the comparison starts. UInt* .
- string2_offset вҖ” The position (zero-based index) in string2 from which the comparison starts. UInt* .
- num_bytes вҖ” The maximum number of bytes to compare in both strings. If string_offset + num_bytes exceeds the end of an input string, num_bytes will be reduced accordingly. UInt* .
Returned value
- -1 вҖ” If string1 [ string1_offset : string1_offset + num_bytes ] < string2 [ string2_offset : string2_offset + num_bytes ].
- 0 вҖ” If string1 [ string1_offset : string1_offset + num_bytes ] = string2 [ string2_offset : string2_offset + num_bytes ].
- 1 вҖ” If string1 [ string1_offset : string1_offset + num_bytes ] > string2 [ string2_offset : string2_offset + num_bytes ].<sep>[RETURN]: - -1 вҖ” If string1 [ string1_offset : string1_offset + num_bytes ] < string2 [ string2_offset : string2_offset + num_bytes ].
- 0 вҖ” If string1 [ string1_offset : string1_offset + num_bytes ] = string2 [ string2_offset : string2_offset + num_bytes ].
- 1 вҖ” If string1 [ string1_offset : string1_offset + num_bytes ] > string2 [ string2_offset : string2_offset + num_bytes ].<sep>[DEMO]: SELECT compareSubstrings('Saxony', 'Anglo-Saxon', 0, 6, 5) AS result, ; в”Ңв”Җresultв”Җв”җ

description: Concatenates the given arguments.
detail:
[DESCRIPTION]: Concatenates the given arguments.<sep>[SYNTAX]: concat(s1, s2, ...)<sep>[ARGUMENTS]: Values of arbitrary type.
Arguments which are not of types String or FixedString are converted to strings using their default serialization. As this decreases performance, it is not recommended to use non-String/FixedString arguments.
Returned values
The String created by concatenating the arguments.
If any of arguments is NULL , the function returns NULL .<sep>[RETURN]: The String created by concatenating the arguments.
If any of arguments is NULL , the function returns NULL .<sep>[DEMO]: SELECT concat('Hello, ', 'World!'); ; в”Ңв”Җconcat('Hello, ', 'World!')в”Җв”җ ; SELECT concat(42, 144);

description:
Like concat but assumes that concat(s1, s2, ...) вҶ’ sn is injective. Can be used for optimization of GROUP BY.
A function is called injective if it returns for different arguments different results. In other words: different arguments never produce identical result.
detail:
[DESCRIPTION]: Like concat but assumes that concat(s1, s2, ...) вҶ’ sn is injective. Can be used for optimization of GROUP BY.
A function is called injective if it returns for different arguments different results. In other words: different arguments never produce identical result.<sep>[SYNTAX]: concatAssumeInjective(s1, s2, ...)<sep>[ARGUMENTS]: Values of type String or FixedString.
Returned values
The String created by concatenating the arguments.
If any of argument values is NULL , the function returns NULL .<sep>[RETURN]: The String created by concatenating the arguments.
If any of argument values is NULL , the function returns NULL .<sep>[DEMO]: в”Ңв”Җconcat(key1, key2)в”Җв”¬в”Җsum(value)в”Җв”җ
example:
в”Ңв”Җconcat(key1, key2)в”Җв”¬в”Җsum(value)в”Җв”җ

description: Concatenates the given strings with a given separator.
detail:
[DESCRIPTION]: Concatenates the given strings with a given separator.<sep>[SYNTAX]: concatWithSeparator(sep, expr1, expr2, expr3...)<sep>[ARGUMENTS]: - sep вҖ” separator. Const String or FixedString .
- exprN вҖ” expression to be concatenated. Arguments which are not of types String or FixedString are converted to strings using their default serialization. As this decreases performance, it is not recommended to use non-String/FixedString arguments.
Returned values
The String created by concatenating the arguments.
If any of the argument values is NULL , the function returns NULL .<sep>[RETURN]: The String created by concatenating the arguments.
If any of the argument values is NULL , the function returns NULL .<sep>[DEMO]: в”Ңв”ҖconcatWithSeparator('a', '1', '2', '3', '4')в”Җв”җ

description:
Like concatWithSeparator but assumes that concatWithSeparator(sep, expr1, expr2, expr3...) вҶ’ result is injective. Can be used for optimization of GROUP BY.
A function is called injective if it returns for different arguments different results. In other words: different arguments never produce identical result.
detail:
[DESCRIPTION]: Like concatWithSeparator but assumes that concatWithSeparator(sep, expr1, expr2, expr3...) вҶ’ result is injective. Can be used for optimization of GROUP BY.
A function is called injective if it returns for different arguments different results. In other words: different arguments never produce identical result.

description: Returns the substring of a string s which starts at the specified byte index offset . Byte counting starts from 1. If offset is 0, an empty string is returned. If offset is negative, the substring starts pos characters from the end of the string, rather than from the beginning. An optional argument length specifies the maximum number of bytes the returned substring may have.
detail:
[DESCRIPTION]: Returns the substring of a string s which starts at the specified byte index offset . Byte counting starts from 1. If offset is 0, an empty string is returned. If offset is negative, the substring starts pos characters from the end of the string, rather than from the beginning. An optional argument length specifies the maximum number of bytes the returned substring may have.<sep>[SYNTAX]: substring(s, offset[, length])<sep>[ARGUMENTS]: - s вҖ” The string to calculate a substring from. String , FixedString or Enum
- offset вҖ” The starting position of the substring in s . (U)Int* .
- length вҖ” The maximum length of the substring. (U)Int* . Optional.
Returned value
A substring of s with length many bytes, starting at index offset . String .<sep>[RETURN]: A substring of s with length many bytes, starting at index offset . String .<sep>[ALIASES]: - substr
- mid
- byteSlice<sep>[DEMO]: в”Ңв”Җdbв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җsubstring('database', 5)в”Җв”¬в”Җsubstring('database', 5, 1)в”Җв”җ

в”Ӯ database в”Ӯ base                     в”Ӯ b                           в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

description:
Returns the substring of a string s which starts at the specified byte index offset for Unicode code points. Byte counting starts from 1 . If offset is 0 , an empty string is returned. If offset is negative, the substring starts pos characters from the end of the string, rather than from the beginning. An optional argument length specifies the maximum number of bytes the returned substring may have.
Assumes that the string contains valid UTF-8 encoded text. If this assumption is violated, no exception is thrown and the result is undefined.
detail:

If offset is negative, the substring starts pos characters from the end of the string, rather than from the beginning. An optional argument length specifies the maximum number of bytes the returned substring may have.
Assumes that the string contains valid UTF-8 encoded text. If this assumption is violated, no exception is thrown and the result is undefined.<sep>[SYNTAX]: substringUTF8(s, offset[, length])<sep>[ARGUMENTS]: - s вҖ” The string to calculate a substring from. String , FixedString or Enum
- offset вҖ” The starting position of the substring in s . (U)Int* .
- length вҖ” The maximum length of the substring. (U)Int* . Optional.
Returned value
A substring of s with length many bytes, starting at index offset .
Implementation details
Assumes that the string contains valid UTF-8 encoded text. If this assumption is violated, no exception is thrown and the result is undefined.<sep>[RETURN]: A substring of s with length many bytes, starting at index offset .
Implementation details
Assumes that the string contains valid UTF-8 encoded text. If this assumption is violated, no exception is thrown and the result is undefined.<sep>[DEMO]: SELECT 'TГӨglich grГјГҹt das Murmeltier.' AS str,
example:
SELECT 'TГӨglich grГјГҹt das Murmeltier.' AS str,
       substringUTF8(str, 9),
       substringUTF8(str, 9, 5)

description: Returns the substring of s before count occurrences of the delimiter delim , as in Spark or MySQL.
detail:
[DESCRIPTION]: Returns the substring of s before count occurrences of the delimiter delim , as in Spark or MySQL.<sep>[SYNTAX]: substringIndex(s, delim, count)<sep>[ARGUMENTS]: - s вҖ” The string to extract substring from. String .
- delim вҖ” The character to split. String .
- count вҖ” The number of occurrences of the delimiter to count before extracting the substring. If count is positive, everything to the left of the final delimiter (counting from the left) is returned. If count is negative, everything to the right of the final delimiter (counting from the right) is returned. UInt or Int<sep>[DEMO]: в”Ңв”ҖsubstringIndex('www.clickhouse.com', '.', 2)в”Җв”җ

description:
This function extracts plain text from HTML or XHTML. It does not conform 100% to the HTML, XML or XHTML specification but the implementation is reasonably accurate and fast. The rules are the following: - Comments are skipped. Example: . Comment must end with --> . Nested comments are disallowed. Note: constructions like and are not valid comments in HTML but they are skipped by other rules. - CDATA is pasted verbatim. Note: CDATA is XML/XHTML-specific and processed on a "best-effort" basis. - script and style elements are removed with all their content. Note: it is assumed that closing tag cannot appear inside content. For example, in JS string literal has to be escaped like "<\/script>" . Note: comments and CDATA are possible inside script or style - then closing tags are not searched inside CDATA. Example: ]]> . But they are still searched inside comments. Sometimes it becomes complicated: var y = "-->"; alert(x + y); Note: script and style can be the names of XML namespaces - then they are not treated like usual script or style elements. Example: Hello . Note: whitespaces are possible after closing tag name: but not before: < / script> . - Other tags or tag-like elements are skipped without inner content. Example: . Note: it is expected that this HTML is illegal: Note: it also skips something like tags: <> , , etc. Note: tag without end is skipped to the end of input: world , Helloworld - there is no whitespace in HTML, but the function inserts it. Also consider: Hello

world

, Hello
world . This behavior is reasonable for data analysis, e.g. to convert HTML to a bag of words. - Also note that correct handling of whitespaces requires the support of 
 and CSS display and white-space properties.
detail:
[DESCRIPTION]: This function extracts plain text from HTML or XHTML. It does not conform 100% to the HTML, XML or XHTML specification but the implementation is reasonably accurate and fast. The rules are the following: - Comments are skipped. Example: . Comment must end with --> . Nested comments are disallowed. Note: constructions like and are not valid comments in HTML but they are skipped by other rules. - CDATA is pasted verbatim. Note: CDATA is XML/XHTML-specific and processed on a "best-effort" basis. - script and style elements are removed with all their content. Note: it is assumed that closing tag cannot appear inside content. For example, in JS string literal has to be escaped like "<\/script>" . Note: comments and CDATA are possible inside script or style - then closing tags are not searched inside CDATA. Example: ]]> . But they are still searched inside comments. Sometimes it becomes complicated: var y = "-->"; alert(x + y); Note: script and style can be the names of XML namespaces - then they are not treated like usual script or style elements. Example: Hello . Note: whitespaces are possible after closing tag name: but not before: < / script> . - Other tags or tag-like elements are skipped without inner content. Example: . Note: it is expected that this HTML is illegal: Note: it also skips something like tags: <> , , etc. Note: tag without end is skipped to the end of input: world , Helloworld - there is no whitespace in HTML, but the function inserts it. Also consider: Hello

world

, Hello
world . This behavior is reasonable for data analysis, e.g. to convert HTML to a bag of words. - Also note that correct handling of whitespaces requires the support of 
 and CSS display and white-space properties.[SYNTAX]: extractTextFromHTML(x)[ARGUMENTS]: - x вҖ” input text. String .
Returned value
- Extracted text. String .[RETURN]: - Extracted text. String .[DEMO]: A text with tags .

description: Compare two strings lexicographically.
detail:
[DESCRIPTION]: Compare two strings lexicographically.<sep>[SYNTAX]: stringCompare(string1, string2[, str1_off, string2_offset, num_bytes]);<sep>[ARGUMENTS]: - string1 вҖ” The first string to compare. String
- string2 - The second string to compare. String
- string1_offset вҖ” The position (zero-based) in string1 from which the comparison starts. Optional, positive number.
- string2_offset вҖ” The position (zero-based index) in string2 from which the comparison starts. Optional, positive number.
- num_bytes вҖ” The maximum number of bytes to compare in both strings. If string_offset + num_bytes exceeds the end of an input string, num_bytes will be reduced accordingly.
Returned value
- -1 вҖ” If string1 [ string1_offset : string1_offset + num_bytes ] < string2 [ string2_offset : string2_offset + num_bytes ] and string1_offset < len( string1 ) and string2_offset < len( string2 ). If string1_offset >= len( string1 ) and string2_offset < len( string2 ).
- 0 вҖ” If string1 [ string1_offset : string1_offset + num_bytes ] = string2 [ string2_offset : string2_offset + num_bytes ] and string1_offset < len( string1 ) and string2_offset < len( string2 ). If string1_offset >= len( string1 ) and string2_offset >= len( string2 ).
- 1 вҖ” If string1 [ string1_offset : string1_offset + num_bytes ] > string2 [ string2_offset : string2_offset + num_bytes ] and string1_offset < len( string1 ) and string2_offset < len( string2 ). If string1_offset < len( string1 ) and string2_offset >= len( string2 ).<sep>[RETURN]: - -1 вҖ” If string1 [ string1_offset : string1_offset + num_bytes ] < string2 [ string2_offset : string2_offset + num_bytes ] and string1_offset < len( string1 ) and string2_offset < len( string2 ). If string1_offset >= len( string1 ) and string2_offset < len( string2 ).
- 0 вҖ” If string1 [ string1_offset : string1_offset + num_bytes ] = string2 [ string2_offset : string2_offset + num_bytes ] and string1_offset < len( string1 ) and string2_offset < len( string2 ). If string1_offset >= len( string1 ) and string2_offset >= len( string2 ).
- 1 вҖ” If string1 [ string1_offset : string1_offset + num_bytes ] > string2 [ string2_offset : string2_offset + num_bytes ] and string1_offset < len( string1 ) and string2_offset < len( string2 ). If string1_offset < len( string1 ) and string2_offset >= len( string2 ).<sep>[DEMO]: в”Ңв”Җresult1в”Җв”¬в”Җresult2в”Җв”¬в”Җresult3в”Җв”җ ; в”Ңв”Җresult2в”Җв”¬в”Җresult1в”Җв”¬в”Җresult3в”Җв”җ

description: Finds all substrings of a given string that have a length of at least n , where the hashes of the (n-1)-grams at the borders of the substring are strictly greater than those of any (n-1)-gram inside the substring. Uses crc32 as a hash function.
detail:
[DESCRIPTION]: Finds all substrings of a given string that have a length of at least n , where the hashes of the (n-1)-grams at the borders of the substring are strictly greater than those of any (n-1)-gram inside the substring. Uses crc32 as a hash function.<sep>[SYNTAX]: sparseGrams(s[, min_ngram_length]);<sep>[ARGUMENTS]: - s вҖ” An input string. String
- min_ngram_length вҖ” The minimum length of extracted ngram. The default and minimal value is 3.
- max_ngram_length вҖ” The maximum length of extracted ngram. The default value is 100. Should be not less than 'min_ngram_length'
Returned value
- An array of selected substrings. Array ( String ).<sep>[RETURN]: - An array of selected substrings. Array ( String ).<sep>[DEMO]: в”Ңв”Җresultв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ

description: Replace part of the string input with another string replace , starting at the 1-based index offset .
detail:
[DESCRIPTION]: Replace part of the string input with another string replace , starting at the 1-based index offset .<sep>[SYNTAX]: overlay(s, replace, offset[, length])<sep>[ARGUMENTS]: - s : A string type String .
- replace : A string type String .
- offset : An integer type Int (1-based). If offset is negative, it is counted from the end of the string s .
- length : Optional. An integer type Int . length specifies the length of the snippet within the input string s to be replaced. If length is not specified, the number of bytes removed from s equals the length of replace ; otherwise length bytes are removed.
Returned value
- A String data type value.<sep>[RETURN]: - A String data type value.<sep>[DEMO]: в”Ңв”Җresв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ ; в”Ңв”Җresв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ

description: Replaces the first occurrence of the substring pattern in haystack by the replacement string.
detail: [DESCRIPTION]: Replaces the first occurrence of the substring pattern in haystack by the replacement string.<sep>[SYNTAX]: replaceOne(haystack, pattern, replacement)

description: Replaces all occurrences of the substring pattern in haystack by the replacement string.
detail: [DESCRIPTION]: Replaces all occurrences of the substring pattern in haystack by the replacement string.<sep>[SYNTAX]: replaceAll(haystack, pattern, replacement)

description:
Replaces the first occurrence of the substring matching the regular expression pattern (in re2 syntax ) in haystack by the replacement string.
replacement can contain substitutions \0-\9 . Substitutions \1-\9 correspond to the 1st to 9th capturing group (submatch), substitution \0 corresponds to the entire match.
To use a verbatim \ character in the pattern or replacement strings, escape it using \ . Also keep in mind that string literals require extra escaping.

description:
Adds a backslash before these characters with special meaning in regular expressions: \0 , \\ , | , ( , ) , ^ , $ , . , [ , ] , ? , * , + , { , : , - .
This implementation slightly differs from re2::RE2::QuoteMeta. It escapes zero byte as \0 instead of \x00 and it escapes only required characters. For more information, see RE2

description: Format the pattern string with the values (strings, integers, etc.) listed in the arguments, similar to formatting in Python. The pattern string can contain replacement fields surrounded by curly braces {} . Anything not contained in braces is considered literal text and copied verbatim into the output. Literal brace character can be escaped by two braces: {{ '{{' }} and {{ '}}' }} . Field names can be numbers (starting from zero) or empty (then they are implicitly given monotonically increasing numbers).

Field names can be numbers (starting from zero) or empty (then they are implicitly given monotonically increasing numbers).<sep>[SYNTAX]: format(pattern, s0, s1, ...)<sep>[DEMO]: SELECT format('{1} {0} {1}', 'World', 'Hello') ; в”Ңв”Җformat('{1} {0} {1}', 'World', 'Hello')в”Җв”җ ; SELECT format('{} {}', 'Hello', 'World')

description: Replaces characters in the string s using a one-to-one character mapping defined by from and to strings. from and to must be constant ASCII strings. If from and to have equal sizes, each occurrence of the 1st character of first in s is replaced by the 1st character of to , the 2nd character of first in s is replaced by the 2nd character of to , etc. If from contains more characters than to , all occurrences of the characters at the end of from that have no corresponding character in to are deleted from s . Non-ASCII characters in s are not modified by the function.

Non-ASCII characters in s are not modified by the function.<sep>[SYNTAX]: translate(s, from, to)<sep>[DEMO]: в”Ңв”Җresв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ ; в”Ңв”Җresв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
